raw_consumer_statement:
Below are the details of the incident : 1.Original Transaction : On XX/XX/year>, I made a {$20.00} payment for transportation services using my card. This transaction was confirmed via a XXXX card reader when I tapped my Citibank credit card. 

2.Unauthorized Charge : A couple of days later, I noticed a charge of {$310.00} from a merchant named XXXX Lux XXXX XXXX XXXX categorized under Beauty and Spas. I did not authorize this charge, nor did I engage in any transaction with this merchant. 

3.Dispute Outcome : Citibank declined my dispute, citing that the charge was processed via a chip reader. However, I have evidence suggesting that the charge amount was deliberately manipulated by the merchant without my consent.

Evidence Supporting My Claim : 1.Upon reviewing the receipt downloaded from the XXXX website, it is evident that someone intentionally customized a charge amount of {$120.00} ( plus tax ) and added an additional {$180.00} as a tip. These amounts were posted to my account without my authorization. I have attached the receipt for your reference. 

XXXX have made multiple attempts to locate contact information for XXXX XXXX  XXXX XXXX and have been unable to do so. This raises serious concerns that the merchant may be engaging in fraudulent practices, potentially targeting consumers to steal credit card information and post unauthorized charges.

This situation has caused me significant distress. As a long-standing Citibank customer , I am deeply disappointed by the decision to favor a merchant whose practices appear fraudulent and unethical. It is unacceptable for a legitimate dispute to be dismissed under these circumstances.
